args.query: SELECT gmap_id, name, description FROM business_description WHERE description ILIKE '%Los Angeles, CA%' LIMIT 10000;

exec_args.sql: SELECT gmap_id, name, description FROM business_description WHERE description ILIKE '%Los Angeles, CA%' LIMIT 10000;

args.query: SELECT gmap_id, AVG(rating) AS average_rating FROM review WHERE gmap_id IN ('gmap_44', 'gmap_41', 'gmap_43', 'gmap_38', 'gmap_39', 'gmap_42', 'gmap_40') GROUP BY gmap_id ORDER BY average_rating DESC LIMIT 5;

exec_args.sql: SELECT gmap_id, AVG(rating) AS average_rating FROM review WHERE gmap_id IN ('gmap_44', 'gmap_41', 'gmap_43', 'gmap_38', 'gmap_39', 'gmap_42', 'gmap_40') GROUP BY gmap_id ORDER BY average_rating DESC LIMIT 5;

args.code:
import pandas as pd

la_businesses = pd.DataFrame(locals()['var_function-call-7244084026481626321'])
average_ratings = pd.DataFrame(locals()['var_function-call-8033909033945671503'])

merged_data = pd.merge(la_businesses, average_ratings, on='gmap_id')

sorted_businesses = merged_data.sort_values(by='average_rating', ascending=False)

top_5_businesses = sorted_businesses.head(5)

result = top_5_businesses[['name', 'average_rating']].to_json(orient='records')
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

sorted_businesses = merged_data.sort_values(by='average_rating', ascending=False)

top_5_businesses = sorted_businesses.head(5)

result = top_5_businesses[['name', 'average_rating']].to_json(orient='records')
